name: StarTrack
description: Community star ratings for movies and TV shows — stored privately on your Jellyfin server.
overview: StarTrack adds a compact star rating pill to every Movie and TV Series detail page in your Jellyfin library. Click it to expand a full panel where you can submit your 1-5 star rating and see a breakdown of every individual user's score. All data lives in a JSON file on your server — nothing leaves your network.

versions.changelog: Major release. Members tab + profile pages with Letterboxd-style stats (histogram, genres, directors, actors, decades, hours watched, calendar heatmap, on-this-day, most rewatched, year cards). Follow graph with followers/following lists. Activity feed across people you follow or everyone. Compare profile UI: taste-similarity score, side-by-side rating distributions, biggest disagreements, and films you both loved. Dedicated Reviews tab. Privacy toggles (hide from members / follower count / following / stats / recent activity) and optional private lists. Diary now shows a clearer rewatch pill with stars right-aligned. Significant performance work — profile and stats endpoints serve cached data instantly while rebuilding fresh data in the background, plus a server-wide item/people cache so heavy users are no longer slow to load. Includes a security pass across endpoints. No data migration needed; existing files are unchanged.

Bug fix: the Rate pill and My Ratings sidebar entry were showing up on Jellyfin's login page and server-picker. New isPreAuthPage() check: hides on any URL matching /login.html, #!/login, #!/addserver, #!/selectserver, #!/wizard, /quickconnect, or when document.body has class 'loginPage', or when no user is logged in yet (no getUserId()). Physically removes the sidebar link + hides the pill so nothing leaks pre-auth.

Actual fix for Letterboxd likes not syncing (v1.4.3 + v1.4.4 were wrong). Root cause: the RSS parser was looking for <letterboxd:liked> but the real field name in Letterboxd RSS is <letterboxd:memberLike> (values 'Yes'/'No'). So entry.Liked was always false and the like catch-up loop found nothing. Verified against davidehrlich's public RSS feed, which ships <letterboxd:memberLike>No</letterboxd:memberLike> on every entry. Parser now reads memberLike (with liked as a forward-compat fallback) so rated+liked entries finally mirror their like into StarTrack. No other code changes required — the 1.4.4 catch-up pass was correct, it just never had any data to work with.

Follow-up to 1.4.3 likes-sync fix: also catch up likes on PREVIOUSLY-SYNCED rated entries. Root cause: SyncRssAsync uses LastSyncedGuid to break out of the walk once it hits a previously-seen entry. So if you rate a film on Monday, sync on Monday (rating + diary come through), then like it on Tuesday and sync on Tuesday — the Monday-rated entry is skipped because its GUID IS the last-synced-guid, and the like is never mirrored. Fixed by adding a separate likes-only pass after the main loop that walks every RSS entry (ignores LastSyncedGuid) and calls AddLikeAsync for any rated+liked entry. AddLikeAsync is idempotent so re-syncing is free. Works for likes added days or weeks after the original rating.

Bug fix from community feedback: when a user rated AND liked a film on Letterboxd, the rating and diary entry synced to StarTrack but the like didn't. Root cause: the RSS parser never read the <letterboxd:liked> field (present on every RSS entry that the user has liked), and the HTML likes scraper is a fragile fallback that doesn't always catch new likes. Now the RSS parser extracts the liked flag and the sync loop calls AddLikeAsync for any rated-and-liked entry. AddLikeAsync is idempotent so re-syncing doesn't create duplicates. The HTML scrape still runs afterwards for pure unrated likes.

Two bug fixes from community feedback against 1.4.1. (1) FIXED: Top 4 favourites row still appeared on Watchlist / Liked / Diary / Reviews / For you / Lists despite the 1.4.1 fix — root cause was a CSS `display:flex!important` rule on `.ir-ov-favs` that beat the inline `style.display='none'` hide. Now toggled via a class with its own `!important` rule. (2) FIXED: Reviews feed ignored every topbar control — type tabs, search box, star filter, sort dropdown all had no effect. `renderReviewsView` now applies the same filter pipeline as the other views (type tab via metadata.type / isAnime, search query against item name, star filter against rating, ten-way sort by date / year / rating / runtime). Also shows a 'N reviews' count in the topbar pill and an empty-state message when filters match nothing.

Three community-requested admin settings. (1) COMMUNITY RECENT MODE — new admin toggle makes the floating Recent pill show recent ratings from EVERY user on the server (with their name next to each entry) instead of only the current user's. (2) HIDE OVERLAY VIEWS — admin can now hide any of Watchlist / Liked / Diary / Reviews / For you / Lists from every user on the server. The Media (films) view is always available. Previously the full seven-view menu was forced on everyone. (3) Top 4 favourites row is now correctly hidden on non-Media views (Watchlist/Liked/Diary/Reviews/For you/Lists) — it was leaking into other views in 1.4.0 where it made no sense. Fixes 'pin a film' / 'pin a series' label not reflecting the active type tab on non-Media views — moot now that the row is only on Media.

Big feature release. (1) 8-LANGUAGE I18N — en, fr, es, de, it, pt, zh, ja. Widget pill + rating panel + My Ratings overlay + admin config page all translate. Translation bundles embedded via WithCulture=false so they survive the satellite-assembly collapse. DOM scrub uses a canonical-English baseline so switching fr→de→en→ja all work without a reload. (2) USER LANGUAGE PICKER — globe button in the rating panel lets each user pick their own language, persisted per-browser in localStorage. (3) USER PREFERENCES MODAL — gear Preferences button in the My Ratings overlay opens a per-user settings dialog: language, hide the floating Rate pill, hide the post-playback rating popup. (4) ADMIN SETTINGS — Dashboard → Plugins → StarTrack now has a full settings form: default language, hide Recent / Letterboxd / Rate-pill floating buttons system-wide, replace native ratings on media detail pages, replace Media Bar plugin ratings, overlay ratings on library posters, post-playback rating popup. (5) POSTER RATING BADGES — gold star badge in the top-right corner of every library card whose item has a StarTrack rating. (6) MEDIA DETAIL RATING SWAP — replaces Jellyfin's native community rating on movie/series detail pages with the StarTrack average + rater count. (7) MEDIA BAR PLUGIN SWAP — if the Media Bar plugin is installed, its rating pills are replaced with StarTrack's average. (8) POST-PLAYBACK RATING POPUP — when a movie or episode finishes, a small prompt asks the user to rate (once per item, skipped if already rated). (9) MY RATINGS POPUP SIZING FIX — works correctly on non-default Jellyfin themes now (width/height:100dvh, transform:none, body lock). ARCHITECTURAL NOTE: Jellyfin strips inline <script> tags from plugin config pages on SPA navigation, so all admin-page JS logic now lives in widget.js which is injected globally via the ScriptInjectionMiddleware.

Films view renamed to 'Media' to reflect the fact that it already covers movies, TV shows, and episodes — the label just implied it was movies-only. New Anime type tab sits alongside All / Movies / TV Shows / Episodes. Anime isn't a real Jellyfin type — the detection is genre/tag based: any item whose Genres or Tags field contains the word 'anime' (case-insensitive) is treated as anime, regardless of whether its underlying type is Movie, Series, or Episode. The metadata batch now pulls Genres + Tags alongside the existing fields, and each item carries an isAnime flag through the hydration pipeline. Top 4 Anime row appears when the Anime tab is active, pulling any pinned favorites that are anime regardless of which underlying-type bucket they live in.

Hygiene pass from a full-plugin audit. All five JSON repositories (ratings, user interactions, diary, lists, Letterboxd settings) now write atomically: serialize to '<file>.tmp' then 'File.Move(..., overwrite: true)'. Previously 'File.WriteAllTextAsync' was used directly on the live file, so a process kill or power loss mid-write would truncate or corrupt the JSON and wipe every rating, review, watchlist entry, diary entry or list on that file. File.Move is atomic on NTFS when source and target are on the same volume, which they are because the temp file is a sibling. No behaviour changes, no new features — just making the persistence layer safe against crashes.

User picker on Everyone's watchlist + full README rewrite. New: when viewing the everyone's-watchlist scope, a user dropdown appears next to the scope buttons letting you filter the aggregated view to a single user (e.g. show only Adam's watchlist). The dropdown is populated from whichever users actually appear in the aggregated data, sorted alphabetically. README rewritten end-to-end to reflect every feature added across the 1.0.x → 1.3.x line: ratings + reviews, full Letterboxd-parity views (Films, Watchlist, Liked, Diary with rewatches, Reviews feed, For You recs, collaborative Lists), per-type Top 4 favourites, full Letterboxd integration (ZIP/CSV import, RSS sync, likes scrape, favorites scrape, CSV export), search + discrete star filter, mobile-responsive UI, client-side metadata cache, and a clear AI-assisted disclosure at the bottom.

Seven fixes and features. (1) FIXED: Top 4 favorites strip occasionally bled into Watchlist/Liked/Diary/Reviews/Lists views — root cause was a stale fetch callback re-showing the row after the user switched views. Added a defensive view check inside the callback. (2) FIXED: list detail page had a horizontal scrollbar from overflowing content; container now word-wraps and respects max-width. (3) FIXED: clicking a film card popped Jellyfin's nav drawer because window.location.hash was being set while the overlay was still closing. New navigateToItem() helper closes the overlay, blurs focus, defers 60ms, and uses Jellyfin's appRouter.show() / Emby.Page.show() before falling back to a hash assignment. (4) FIXED: mobile Letterboxd panel inputs overflowed the panel border — added box-sizing: border-box on every child of .ir-ov-lb-row in the mobile media query. (5) FIXED: For-you recommendations excluded TV series — query now includes both BaseItemKind.Movie and BaseItemKind.Series, and the path-exists check accepts Directory.Exists too (Series have folder paths, not file paths). (6) NEW: delete-list buttons. The list detail page shows a 'Delete list' button to the owner; the list cards in the index show an X icon on hover (also owner-only). Confirmation prompt before deletion. (7) NEW: 'Everyone's watchlist' view inside the Watchlist tab — toggle pills at the top let you flip between 'My watchlist' and a server-wide aggregated view sorted by how many users want each title. New endpoint GET /Plugins/StarTrack/EveryonesWatchlist returns each item with the list of users who have it. Cards show 'N users: zack, adam, mum +X' on the everyone view.

Six fixes and features. (1) FIXED: rewatches via Sync now didn't show up in diary — SyncRssAsync only wrote to ratings, not the diary store. Now writes a diary entry for every imported RSS rating with the rewatch flag parsed from the letterboxd:rewatch RSS field. Re-syncing dedupes on (itemId, day). (2) Top 4 favorites is now bound to the active type tab — clicking 'Movies' shows Top 4 Movies, 'TV Shows' shows Top 4 Series, 'Episodes' shows Top 4 Episodes. The 'All' tab still shows all populated rows. Type-tab clicks now refresh the favorites strip too. (3) Create-list modal — replaces the cascade of window.prompt() calls with a proper modal: name field, description textarea, collaborative checkbox, focus + Enter to confirm, animated backdrop, success toast. (4) Reviews feed tab — new ✍ Reviews view in the topbar shows every rating with a non-empty review across the whole server, rendered as a vertical feed with poster, film name, reviewer, star bar, date, and the review text in an italic blockquote-style block. (5) Mobile responsive CSS — full @media (max-width: 768px) and (max-width: 380px) overhaul. The topbar wraps cleanly, search/filter become full-width, view tabs become horizontally scrollable, the favorites grid drops to 2 columns, the Letterboxd panel buttons stack instead of overflowing, modals fit within the viewport, and hover-actions become always-visible since touch devices have no hover state. (6) Top 4 episodes pin row added (was Movies + Series only).

Big bug-fix bundle from log analysis. (1) FIXED: likes.csv was never importing because Letterboxd packs it inside likes/films.csv (a subfolder), not at the ZIP root. The CSV finder now matches both bare filename and full path. (2) FIXED: Letterboxd 403 on watchlist RSS and likes scrape — the default .NET HttpClient User-Agent is blocked by Letterboxd's anti-bot. Now sends a real Chrome 124 UA + proper Accept headers, so all three Letterboxd fetches (favorites scrape, watchlist RSS, likes scrape) work consistently. (3) FIXED: import counters were lying — 'skipped' lumped 'film not in library' with 'already on watchlist'. Now logged separately as alreadyOnWatchlist + notInLibrary so you can see what's actually happening. (4) STAR FILTER is now discrete — All ratings, 5★, 4.5★, 4★, 3.5★, ... down to 0.5★ — match exactly one star value rather than 'and up' ranges. (5) DIARY entries now show a real visual 5-star bar (filled / half / empty glyphs) instead of a number, exactly as requested. Half stars use a CSS clip-path overlay. (6) ADD-TO-LIST now opens a proper modal dialog — clickable list rows with film counts, inline 'create new list' input, focus + Enter to confirm, success toast on add. No more window.prompt. (7) TOP 4 is now per-type — Top 4 Movies, Top 4 Series, Top 4 Episodes. The Movies row always shows; the Series and Episode rows only appear when you've pinned at least one item of that type. Pin cap raised from 4 to 12 (4 of each type). When you click the star-pin button on a series, it goes into the Series row, not Movies.

Polish + bug-fix bundle on top of 1.3.0. (1) FIXED: 'Newest rated' sort returning random order — root cause was the Letterboxd CSV importer setting RatedAt to DateTime.UtcNow for every row, so every imported rating clustered at the same instant and the sort comparator couldn't distinguish them. Now parses the Date column from ratings.csv and uses the original Letterboxd timestamp. The sort comparator is also defensive against null/NaN dates and falls back to alphabetical title sort on ties. (2) FIXED: Sync now didn't pull likes — Letterboxd has no public RSS feed for likes, so the new SyncLikesScrapeAsync fetches the /username/likes/films/ HTML page and parses the poster grid via regex. Wired into SyncRssAsync alongside the existing watchlist sync. One Sync now click now gets ratings + watchlist + likes. (3) NEW: hover actions on every grid card — pin to favorites, add to a list. The card highlights as you hover and the actions slide in from the top-right. Add-to-list opens a numbered prompt of your existing lists with an option to create a new one. (4) NEW: X button on Top 4 favorite slots — hover any filled slot to reveal a red × that removes the film from the slot. (5) NEW: star tier badges with color differentiation — 4.5+ gets bright gold + glow, 4-4.4 gold, 3-3.9 silver-blue, 2-2.9 bronze, below 2 muted red. Makes the grid scannable at a glance. Safe in-place upgrade from 1.3.0; no schema changes.

Polish + major features. (1) DIARY now has its own storage with rewatch support — import diary.csv from the Letterboxd ZIP and rewatches of the same film show up as separate entries grouped by month, with dates and review text visible per row. New endpoints: GET /MyDiary, POST /Diary, DELETE /Diary/{id}. (2) STAR FILTER dropdown in the topbar — filter any view by rating range (4.5+, 4+, 3+, 2+, below 3). (3) RECOMMENDATIONS reshuffle — big red button at the top of the For you view that rerolls 30 picks from a 60-candidate pool, so different films show up every click. (4) EXPORT CSV button in the topbar — downloads your ratings as a Letterboxd-compatible ratings.csv (Date, Name, Year, Rating) for backup, migration, or round-tripping back to Letterboxd. New GET /ExportCsv endpoint. (5) SYNC NOW now also pulls your Letterboxd watchlist RSS feed — one click syncs ratings AND watchlist additions. (6) IMPORT TOP 4 button in the Letterboxd panel — scrapes your public Letterboxd profile page for the 'favourite films' section and sets them as StarTrack favorites. (7) TOP 4 FAVORITES row is now always visible on the Films view with 4 numbered placeholder slots when empty, so the feature is discoverable. Enhanced hint text. (8) COLLABORATIVE LISTS — new Lists view in the overlay. Create a list, add films, and other users on the server can also contribute to collaborative lists. New endpoints: GET /Lists, POST /Lists, DELETE /Lists/{id}, POST /Lists/{id}/Items, DELETE /Lists/{id}/Items/{itemId}. (9) PERFORMANCE — client-side metadata cache means switching between views is instant after the first load. No more re-fetching the same Jellyfin items every tab click. New storage files: diary.json, lists.json. Existing ratings.json and user_interactions.json unchanged. Safe in-place upgrade from 1.2.0.

Big feature release. (1) WATCHLIST — new bookmark button on the rating pill + Watchlist view in the overlay. Also imported from Letterboxd's watchlist.csv automatically when you drop the export ZIP in. (2) LIKED FILMS — a heart toggle on the rating pill separate from star rating (Letterboxd-style). Imports from likes.csv. New Liked view in the overlay. (3) DIARY VIEW — chronological list of everything you've rated, forced newest-first, with dates and reviews visible per card. (4) TOP 4 FAVORITES — pin up to 4 films as your profile favorites with a dedicated row at the top of the Films view. Fifth pin prompts to replace the oldest. (5) RECOMMENDATIONS — new 'For you' tab that computes your genre preferences from your existing ratings (weighted by star count) and surfaces unrated films in your top-3 genres, sorted by community rating. Uses Jellyfin's existing library query, no external calls. (6) SEARCH — live search input in the overlay topbar, filters the active view by title substring with a 150ms debounce. All six features work together in a single unified My Ratings overlay with view tabs, type sub-filters, sort dropdown, and search. New endpoints: /Interactions/{itemId}, /MyWatchlist, /Watchlist/{itemId}, /MyLikes, /Likes/{itemId}, /MyFavorites, /Recommendations. New storage file at /InternalRating/user_interactions.json. All existing endpoints and ratings.json data unchanged — safe in-place upgrade from 1.1.6.

Zombie library item filter + dead ratings cleanup. User reported that after a hard drive failure, Jellyfin kept zombie DB entries for movies whose files no longer exist on disk — the rating pill matched to these zombies, so imports succeeded but the My Ratings grid showed broken thumbnails for unplayable items. Two fixes: (1) BuildMovieLookup now skips items whose Path doesn't resolve via File.Exists, so new imports only ever land on real files. Diagnose UI shows how many zombies were filtered. Network mounts that are temporarily unreachable are still treated as alive so a momentary blip doesn't wipe the user's library. (2) New 🗑 Clean dead ratings button in the overlay Letterboxd panel iterates every existing StarTrack rating, checks whether the referenced item still exists and has a living path, and deletes any that don't. Returns a count of rows + items removed. Confirmation prompt before running. New POST /Plugins/StarTrack/Letterboxd/Cleanup endpoint exposes the same operation.

THE fix for the 113 ambiguous imports. Real-world diagnostic from a 2,651-movie library showed that 1,065 movies shared a normalized title with at least one other movie (mostly duplicate 4K/HD copies across libraries, plus genuine franchise collisions like 'King Kong' 1933/1976/2005). The old matcher bailed with 'ambiguous' whenever the year disambiguation didn't pick a unique winner, so 113 out of 154 CSV rows silently dropped. New matcher ALWAYS picks a winner: exact year → closest year → oldest year → first candidate. Multiple library copies of the same film now always match. Also fixes the Diagnose button showing 'Library query returned undefined movies' — Jellyfin's default JSON serializer isn't camelCase, so the client's d.libraryMovieCount was always undefined. Fixed by adding explicit JsonPropertyName attrs on every DTO property forcing camelCase, plus a defensive camelCase/PascalCase accessor in the client. Diagnose UI now also shows unique-normalized-title count so you can see how much collision you have upfront.

Diagnostic tooling for Letterboxd sync. New 🔍 Diagnose button in the My Ratings overlay runs the same library query the matcher uses and shows you the total movie count, whether the fallback query was needed, and the first 20 movies with both their original and normalized titles — so you can see at a glance whether StarTrack can see your library and how your titles are being normalized. New GET /Plugins/StarTrack/Letterboxd/Diagnose endpoint returns this data as JSON for scripting. Also adds INFO-level request logs at the top of the Import and SyncNow endpoints so you can verify in the Jellyfin server log that the request is actually reaching the plugin.

Letterboxd matcher rewritten. Previous versions ran a separate library query per CSV row with InternalItemsQuery.Years as a filter, which silently returned 0 results on some Jellyfin setups and was also slow. New approach: fetch every Movie from the library ONCE per import, build an in-memory dictionary keyed by normalized title, then match everything against that dictionary. Much faster and handles year drift cleanly (exact year → ±1 year fallback). Also adds a library fallback query that re-fetches without type filter and filters in-memory by GetBaseItemKind — covers the case where IncludeItemTypes returns nothing. The import result now includes libraryMovieCount, and the status line in both the overlay and the small widget shows it next to the import summary so you can immediately see whether the library query is working at all. Every unmatched title is logged at Debug level with its normalized form so you can diagnose matching gaps from the Jellyfin server logs.

Three Letterboxd UX fixes reported against 1.1.1. (1) Letterboxd settings are now also available from the big 'My Ratings' full-page overlay — new gold '⚙ Letterboxd' button next to Close opens an inline settings panel with the username field, auto-sync toggle, Save / Sync now buttons, and file upload, so you don't have to dig into the small rating pill to configure sync. (2) CSV import now accepts the Letterboxd export ZIP directly — just drag in the .zip you downloaded from letterboxd.com, the server extracts ratings.csv (with diary.csv as a fallback) automatically. No more manually extracting the archive. (3) Title matching is far more forgiving: strips diacritics so 'Amélie' matches 'Amelie', normalizes leading/trailing 'The/A/An' so 'The Matrix' matches 'Matrix, The', and treats apostrophes / colons / Unicode dashes / curly quotes / ampersands as word separators. Sync and import also surface a list of unmatched titles under the status line so you can see exactly which films from Letterboxd weren't found in your library. No schema/endpoint changes, safe in-place upgrade from 1.1.1.

Letterboxd sync UX fixes: (1) replaced the floating gear icon with an inline '⚙ Letterboxd sync' button at the bottom of both the item-rating view and the recent-ratings view, so it no longer overlaps the 'View all →' button and is always clearly discoverable; (2) fixed the back button getting stuck on the Letterboxd view — now always restores the correct primary view based on whether a movie is currently selected; (3) fixed the rating pill not reappearing when navigating from admin dashboard back to home — the hash cache is now invalidated when we hide on admin/video pages so the next nav check re-runs cleanly; (4) fixed the 'stale black bar' that persisted when the panel was closed with the Letterboxd view open — both the click-outside handler and the next pill-open now reset the view state explicitly. No schema/endpoint changes, safe in-place upgrade from 1.1.0.

Letterboxd sync — each user can now connect their Letterboxd account and pull their ratings into StarTrack. Click the gear icon in the rating panel on any movie to open Letterboxd settings: paste your Letterboxd username, toggle hourly auto-sync (pulls new ratings from your public RSS feed), or upload your Letterboxd CSV export for a full-history one-shot import. Films are matched to your Jellyfin library by title + year; unmatched films are reported back. A new 'StarTrack Letterboxd Sync' scheduled task runs hourly in Dashboard → Scheduled Tasks. Also: hide the floating rating pill on admin dashboard, server settings, metadata editor, scheduled tasks, plugins catalog, library settings, user edit and per-user preferences pages — the pill now only shows on Movie/Series/Episode detail pages. The sidebar nav entry still appears everywhere. Adds MIT LICENSE and third-party attribution notes.

Fix: stars badge on My Ratings cards now uses a solid near-black background with bright gold text and a gold border — readable over any poster image. Card title and meta text also have stronger drop-shadows for legibility.

Redesign: red/black theme throughout My Ratings — dark topbar with red accent border, boxed pill tab buttons (All/Movies/TV Shows/Episodes) with red active state, red play-circle on card hover, red stars badge. Repo cleanup: removed Tampermonkey script, old File Transformation code, and stale ZIPs.

Fix: sidebar 'My Ratings' link now injects by finding the navMenuSection parent container directly — works with all Jellyfin sidebar customisations including KefinTweaks. Redesign: My Ratings cards are now Letterboxd-style poster-only with gradient overlay, stars badge, and a play-arrow reveal on hover. Topbar separated from scroll area for cleaner layout.

Fix: sidebar 'My Ratings' link now injects reliably using broader nav selectors and inserts before the User section. Fix: rating pill no longer appears during video playback. New: My Ratings is now a solid-background full page (no see-through). New: tab buttons (All / Movies / TV Shows / Episodes) to filter your library by type. The sort dropdown applies within each tab.

New: personal ratings library overlay — click 'My Ratings' in the sidebar to open a full-screen Letterboxd-style grid of everything you've rated with 10 sort options (newest/oldest rated, film year ↑↓, your rating ↑↓, avg rating ↑↓, length ↑↓). New: 'My Ratings' sidebar link injected into the Jellyfin nav. Fix: recent ratings pill on home screen now shows only your own ratings instead of all users.

New: text reviews — add an optional written review when submitting a star rating. Click a username in the ratings list to expand their review. New: recent ratings panel — when browsing the home screen or library (no item selected), the widget shows a live feed of the latest ratings across all users with item names and review previews. Fix: page badge now injects on its own row above the IMDb/MDBList badges instead of colliding inline.

Fix: compile against Jellyfin.Controller 10.11.6 (exact match for server version) instead of 10.11.7 which caused NotSupported/plugin load failure due to ABI mismatch. All v1.0.10 fixes included: IAuthorizationContext for user ID, no IUserManager.GetUserById, net9.0 target.

Breaking fix: upgraded from Jellyfin 10.9/net8 to Jellyfin 10.11/net9 to match current Jellyfin releases. Removed IUserManager.GetUserById call which caused MissingMethodException on Jellyfin 10.10+ (method signature changed). Username now resolved from JWT claims instead. Ratings saving should now work on Jellyfin 10.11.x.

Fix ratings saving: switched to IAuthorizationContext (Jellyfin-native API) to resolve user ID — eliminates 401 errors on POST/DELETE. Fix half-star visual with clip-path instead of overflow:hidden. Add Episode support (widget now shows on individual episode detail pages). Improve save error logging in browser console.

Fix ratings saving: corrected user ID claim lookup to use ClaimTypes.NameIdentifier so POST/DELETE endpoints correctly identify the user. Added half-star ratings (click left/right half of each star for .5 increments). Added explicit Submit button — stars select a pending rating, button sends it. Added flash feedback on save (green success/red error). Added average rating pill next to IMDb badge on detail page.

Complete injection rewrite: replaced File Transformation reflection approach with ASP.NET Core IStartupFilter + response middleware. The middleware intercepts every index.html HTTP response and injects the widget script tag directly — no file system permissions needed, no third-party plugins required. File Transformation plugin is no longer needed.

Fix: File Transformation registration now happens synchronously inside StartAsync instead of on a background thread. File Transformation finalises its callback list early during startup — registering on a background thread meant we always missed the window. This is why JavaScript Injector worked (it registers synchronously) but StarTrack did not.

Diagnostics: callback invocation counter, status, payload type and contents length now shown in /Plugins/StarTrack/Debug. Console.log added to widget.js so browser devtools shows whether the script loads and what item IDs it detects.

Fix: TransformationPatches.SetContents now wraps the modified HTML in a JValue before assigning it back to the JObject payload, fixing the silent ArgumentException that prevented File Transformation from injecting the script tag. Also: debug report now shows the real assembly version instead of hardcoded string.

Fix: File Transformation now receives a proper JObject payload (built via reflection from the already-loaded Newtonsoft.Json assembly) instead of a POCO that caused a cast exception. This is the root cause of the widget not appearing when index.html is read-only.

Diagnostic: added /Plugins/StarTrack/Debug endpoint to report injection status without needing server logs. Injection now tries multiple known Linux web paths (/usr/share/jellyfin/web, /usr/lib/jellyfin/web, etc.) in addition to the configured WebPath. Retries injection after 5 s delay. All injection log messages upgraded to Warning level so they are always visible. File Transformation registration now logs available method names if RegisterTransformation is not found.

Fix: removed Newtonsoft.Json dependency that was preventing the plugin from loading. Added File Transformation plugin integration for non-destructive index.html injection. Fixed web path resolution using IApplicationPaths.WebPath.
Initial release — star ratings for Movies and TV Shows, community average display, expandable per-user score list, and remove-rating support.
